title: LocalTalker
summary: A Blueprint-first Unreal plugin for local character conversations with on-device llama.cpp generation and Qwen3-TTS speech.
description: LocalTalker is a late-era systems city focused on real-time local NPC conversation inside Unreal Engine. It combines in-process llama.cpp text generation, a persistent Qwen3-TTS worker, and game-facing conversation orchestration into one plugin-shaped product.

What makes it notable is that it is not just a model wrapper. The project includes the player interaction layer, turn-taking and proximity grouping, local voice pipeline management, and automation tests needed to make spoken character interaction feel like an actual gameplay system.

media.alt: A forest stronghold representing the LocalTalker Unreal plugin.
media.caption: A dialogue stronghold built for local NPC conversations, speech synthesis, and in-engine orchestration.

greatWorks.title: On-Device Dialogue Director
greatWorks.summary: A local conversation stack for Unreal characters that streams tokens, speaks replies, and manages who talks when.

relationships.label: Broader AI systems and future-tech arc
Public-facing proof of fast AI experimentation

highlights: Builds a Blueprint-first Unreal plugin instead of a standalone AI demo.
Runs local text generation and local speech synthesis as a game-facing conversation stack.
Includes proximity grouping, turn-taking, player interaction routing, and automation coverage.
productionQueue: Keep tightening latency, interruption handling, and spoken turn flow inside Unreal.
Expand the packaging and setup path so the plugin is easier to move between projects.
Push further on believable local character interaction as a reusable gameplay system.

features: Streaming token generation with in-process llama.cpp
Conversation director and player interaction components
architectureNotes: Treats local character conversation as a reusable Unreal gameplay subsystem rather than a chat prototype.
Pairs runtime components with automation tests, voice-path configuration, and packaged-worker tooling.
